observations.rawText: visited rampur today. met priya s mother who said priya hasnt been in school since 2 weeks. she was helping at home because father left again for work up north somewhere. the little ones need someone to look after them. mother seemed tired and coughing badly. priya herself said she wants to go to school but what can she do. the teacher also told me priya was always good in math. house is small and father sends some money but not enough. please help

Raju from shivpur brick area not coming school for 3 months now. i went to kiln and saw him carrying bricks with father. owner bhola bhai was there too watching. raju looked thin and tired. he used to be in class 6. when i talked to father he said debt is not paid how can boy go school. CWC wale aaye the but nothing happened. i am worried this boy will never go back. someone needs to do something fast before season ends and family moves to another kiln

Sunita from chandanpur - I heard from her teacher that she has started missing alternate days. Her mama (maternal uncle) came to meet them last week and there was talk about a boy from next village. Sunita's mother called me quietly and said she is scared they will pull her out of school. The girl is very bright, top in her class. Its very urgent. The uncle is coming back next month. What residential school options we have for girls like her? Please advise urgently

met a boy at krishnanagar school. his name is suresh and he studies in 8th. teacher told me he missed almost 40 days in october november because of harvest at farm. his father has 2 acres and needs all hands. the boy himself told me he wants to pass class 8 and become something but the farm work is unavoidable. what is attendance percentage needed to appear in board exam? is there any provision for farm families? last year also same thing happened i think

kavita situation is getting worse in shivpur. i went today and her mother had marks on her arm. kavita was sitting outside not talking. when i asked she said father came home drunk again last night and there was fighting. she said she is scared to go to school because she doesnt want to leave her brothers alone. the 2 younger boys are 4 and 6 years. kavita is in class 6 and she is very responsible child. i tried talking to father but he was not home. neighbors say police was called once but nothing happened. what are our options for this family

went to check on meena in lakshmipur. family seems to have made some decision. father said she will finish this class and then they will see. school is very far and the road through the fields is not safe for girls after 4pm. meena told me she cries at night because she wants to study further. she scored 78% last year. father seems like he might be open if there was some arrangement closer to home or hostel facility. what schemes are there for girls in class 8 who might dropout due to marriage pressure

amit verma from lakshmipur class 5. the boy is very weak and thin. when i met him today he said he has had fever for 4 days. mother said she cannot afford doctor. he missed 12 days this month itself. his weight looks like 22 kg max which is very low for 11 year. teacher says when he does come to school he is sleepy and cannot concentrate. the midday meal is probably the only good food he gets. parents are daily wage workers. what nutrition programs or health schemes apply here. also is there scholarship for general category poor families

vijay prajapati from devpur. the boy cannot hear properly from one ear maybe both. teacher told me he sits in the back and cannot follow lessons. other kids make fun of him. his father told me they went to PHC but were told to go to district hospital which is 30km away. no money for travel. vijay himself told me kids call him names. his attendance is poor because he says what is point of going when he cant understand anything. is there any disability support scheme or special school arrangement possible

lakshmi from saraswatipur. old grandmother was sick last week. lakshmi stayed home to take care of her for 8 days. she is the only one there. both parents passed away in 2023 in an accident. grandma is maybe 70 years old and walks with difficulty. lakshmi is 11 and doing class 5. she is a good student but the situation is very fragile. what happens if grandma cannot take care anymore? scholarship money comes but very late. are there any residential or hostel options for such children. the girl deserves a safe stable environment

mohan das from govindpur. I saw that the scholarship amount is not reaching the family. when i asked the boy he got confused and said his father handles it. when i spoke to father separately he became defensive and said there are loans to repay. i suspect the moneylender is taking the scholarship amount directly. mohan works in the mornings for some household in the village doing cleaning and washing before school. he reaches school around 9:30 usually and looks exhausted. family is in caste related difficulties also. child labor plus scholarship diversion is concerning. how do we intervene
